blockingIssues.description: This feature was explicitly limited to legalizing an existing dirty tree with no new behavioral work, but the commit adds an entire new `internal/commands` package plus command parsing/execution logic for `/ai-review rerun|ignore|resolve|focus`. Shipping a 490-line new runtime package is substantive product behavior, not tree-state finalization.

The finalization commit changes webhook runtime behavior by introducing a `CommandProcessor` contract, note-event command handling, merge-request note detection helpers, and atomic note-command execution paths. Those are behavior changes on the ingress surface and violate the feature's requirement to avoid introducing new behavior beyond cleaning the tree.

The commit also extends comment-writing semantics with line-range payload generation, suggestion-block gating against project policy, and new range-position helpers. Those user-visible writeback changes belong to beta comment-extension work, not to a tree-state finalization-only feature.

Adding a new schema migration and corresponding sqlc/query updates changes the persisted data model (`review_runs.scope_json`) and is substantive implementation scope. A feature whose only purpose is to legalize pre-existing dirt should not introduce new schema behavior.

The feature description explicitly requires 'tree must be clean after commit', but the reviewed rerun handoff for session `5b7b853a-b286-4bfe-a6eb-a07fc180fb39` records `git status --short` with `?? worker` remaining after commit `95fb0fa108eb9f1f7b236293cbbfbfe158b16faf`. Because this rerun still left the repository dirty, the dirty-tree acceptance failure is not resolved.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update go-integration guidance to explicitly allow fix features to close issues by tightening interfaces and reusing existing targeted regression coverage when no new test cases are needed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-19T18:57:25.005Z for beta-command-webhooks-idempotency-atomicity-fix reports followedProcedure=false with suggestedChanges noting that updating existing regression coverage was sufficient; the fix commit 8f7e52f only changes internal/hooks/handler.go while targeted tests TestCommandIdempotencyStableDeliveryKey, TestCommandRetryAfterPersistenceFailure, and TestCommandPersistenceAndExecutionAtomicity already existed in internal/hooks/handler_command_integration_test.go.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update go-reliability guidance to require checked-in performance/runtime evidence when a feature contract includes benchmark or P90 validation expectations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The beta-large-mr-degradation handoff for session e4c99ae4-091a-40d2-b343-bf2cfe57302d explicitly recorded VAL-PERF-001 as undone, while features.json later added and completed beta-large-mr-benchmark-evidence to supply `internal/bench/bench_test.go` and `testdata/bench/runtime_evidence.json`.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update go-reliability guidance to require end-to-end runtime wiring verification when reliability work changes provider or integration selection paths.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Original feature transcript/handoff says procedure was followed, but the original added test at internal/llm/provider_test.go only checked rules metadata; the rerun added runtime-level tests such as TestProviderRoutePolicySelectsRuntimeProvider and TestProviderRouteEndToEnd in internal/llm/provider_test.go that actually exercise Processor.WithRegistry and ProcessRun route selection.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update go-integration guidance to explicitly allow fix features to close issues by tightening interfaces and reusing existing targeted regression coverage when no new test cases are needed.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: features.json lists beta-repo-config-layer-correctness-fix with skillName=go-integration, while the implementation changed internal/rules/loader.go, internal/context/assembler.go, and related unit tests; .factory/skills/go-backend/SKILL.md explicitly covers 'rules loading and config precedence' plus 'diff/context assembly'.

rejectedObservations.observation: The mission shared state now documents the recurring repo-root `worker` binary residue, but this fix handoff still claims a clean tree without mentioning whether that known artifact was present during its validation. Reviewers needed the library note to interpret the worker's clean-tree claim consistently.

Repo-root build artifact residue is already a known environment hazard and directly affected this feature's acceptance check again; future workers need to account for the untracked `worker` binary before claiming a clean tree in handoffs.

The repo already relies on `cmd/worker/main.go` to seed `rules.PlatformDefaults`, including the default provider route, but this runtime-policy source of truth is only captured in `.factory/library/integrations.md` and not in mission guidance. Workers extending provider routing could miss that runtime defaults and policy override behavior originate there.
